name: Guinea-Bissau
avoid: June to October is rainy, roads to Cacheu get slower, and sea crossings to the Bijagós become much less predictable
Ferry and pirogue schedules are loose even in the dry season, so keep buffer nights in Bissau or Bubaque instead of a same-day international connection
Cash is essential outside bigger Bissau hotels; many islands have no reliable ATM, card machine or backup fuel if you change plans late
stopoverNote: Bissau (OXB) is the only practical international gateway; most trips work best as Bissau city time first, then a ferry or pirogue from Porto de Pidjiguiti to Bubaque for the Bijagós, with Cacheu added as a dry-season road day trip or simple overnight.

cities.name: Bissau
cities.notes: Compact capital for colonial facades, Bandim market energy, port history and the logistics every Guinea-Bissau trip runs through

cities.name: Bijagós Archipelago
cities.notes: Island chain reached via Bubaque, with pirogue transfers, beach lodges, birdlife and Orango's rare saltwater hippos

cities.name: Cacheu
cities.notes: Historic river town with a small Portuguese fort, mangrove scenery and a slavery-history stop most travelers tackle from Bissau

itinerary.sim: Orange and MTN are the most practical local picks in Bissau, but island coverage is patchy and often drops to voice-only outside Bubaque; budget roughly ₹700–₹1,400 for a starter SIM or eSIM with enough data for maps, messages and light hotspot use.

days.theme: Ease into Guinea-Bissau with old Bissau walks, Bandim market noise and the fortress that anchors the country's modern history

activities.name: Airport taxi from Osvaldo Vieira International Airport into central Bissau
Fortaleza d'Amura guide or permission contribution when access is allowed
Bissau Velho and Pidjiguiti waterfront walk
Candonga hops across town
Taxi rides between Bandim Market, the fort area and your hotel

days.theme: Use a second day for culture and practical errands before the islands, with enough slack for ferry timing and slow city logistics

activities.name: Museu Nacional Etnográfico entry or donation
Bandim Market browsing and snack stop
Taxi to Porto de Pidjiguiti to reconfirm Bijagós ferry or pirogue seats
Short in-city taxi across Bissau
Bissau waterfront sunset walk

days.theme: Cross from Bissau to Bubaque, settle into island time and keep day one light because boats run on weather and patience

activities.name: Consulmar ferry or public pirogue from Bissau to Bubaque
Shared pirogue harbour transfer to nearby lodge beaches or Rubane
Bubaque town and beach walk
Local taxi between the quay and your guesthouse when available
Sunset drinks or light dinner by the water

days.theme: Spend a full day on the water with a pirogue and keep the plan flexible around tides, wind and whichever island your lodge can reach best

activities.name: Shared Bijagós island-hopping pirogue day trip
Short beach landing or village guide contribution
Rubane or sandbank stopover
Extra fuel surcharge for longer private boat routing if seas are rough
Return pirogue at sunset

days.theme: Make the signature wildlife day to Orango, where timing the tide and guide availability matters more than starting early for its own sake

activities.name: Shared pirogue from Bubaque or Rubane toward Orango
Orango National Park entry
Guided Orango Island hippo-watching excursion
Community guide or ranger contribution
Simple seafood lunch at lodge or village stop

days.theme: Do Cacheu as a dry-season history day with river views, a fort stop and the understanding that most travelers sleep back in Bissau

activities.name: Bissau-Cacheu candonga or shared minibus round-trip
Cacheu Fort entry
Local guide or museum-room tip at the fort
Cacheu River pirogue or canoe outing
Short taxi hop between the road stop, fort and waterfront
